The program was carried out.
Within ten minutes after the cOun-

But labor didn't go home and Mr.
Coates returned with word that un
less a definite hearing was prom

some few rights, and it was decided
that the special session tonight
would be devoted to the wage and
matron problems. The only differ.
ence being that while labor and the
woman's club will be on hand to,
night and may get what they want,
labor has been convinced that the
recall is the only way to get clean
government in this city, and no fu,
ture action of the council is going
to change the idea of labor about
this.


There were more than the prom
Used 5000 men in the labor parade
last night. According to the best
estimates there were nearly 5000
but at the least calculation a third
of Spokane's voters last night
marched to the city hall, and saw
councilmen insult their leaders
their cause and their plea for jus
tice.


And that, in the long rum" is the
best thing that ever happened IN
Spokane for good government.
